results.caption:
They didn't just build ransomware. They built a brand.

DragonForce emerged December 2023 with a logo, a manifesto, and a business model that would pull in 363 victims across 30+ countries.

This wasn't a hacker. This was a cartel.

The circuit dragon is real. And it's still recruiting affiliates today.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #RaaS #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: A mythological predator reimagined as digital infrastructure — the dragon IS the threat, and viewers can't look away.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
The moment you see this note — it's already too late.

DragonForce's ransom notes carry a signature. ChaCha8 encryption. Cross-platform reach. A negotiation portal on the dark web. A deadline.

Behind every note: a BYOVD attack that blinded your EDR using truesight.sys before you even knew they were inside.

212.5% attack surge in one year. This is what industrialized ransomware looks like.

#RansomNote #DragonForce #CyberCrime #IncidentResponse #DFIR
results.hook: The ransom note as crime scene evidence — real technical artifacts under forensic light make cybersecurity viscerally real for every viewer.

results.caption:
No confirmed nation-state. No identified ringleader. No attribution.

DragonForce operates in the shadows of the shadows — a cartel structure designed specifically to obscure origin, diffuse accountability, and frustrate investigators.

Malaysia? A false flag? A coalition? Nobody knows.

The most dangerous threat actor is the one you can't name.

#ThreatAttribution #DragonForce #CyberEspionage #UnknownThreat #CyberSecurity
results.hook: Unknown attribution is the most unnerving fact in cybersecurity — a shadow with no face drives maximum psychological engagement.

results.caption:
December 6, 2023. A new post appeared on BreachForums.

It offered affiliates 80% of every ransom paid. A cross-platform encryptor. EDR-killing drivers. Dark web infrastructure. A brand.

In that moment, DragonForce went from zero to cartel.

Within 18 months: 363 victims. £300M+ in damages. Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods.

It started with a forum post.

#BreachForums #DarkWeb #DragonForce #RaaS #CyberCrime
results.hook: A specific date — December 6, 2023 — gives audiences a timestamp for evil, making the abstract concrete and shareable.

results.caption:
March 19, 2025. DragonForce didn't just run ransomware.

They announced a cartel.

A formal restructuring. An absorbed RansomHub operation. A new governance model for cybercrime. Complete with branding, affiliate agreements, and a revenue model that would make an MBA jealous.

This is what ransomware looks like when it grows up.

363 victims. £300M+ impact. And they called a press conference to celebrate.

#DragonForceCartel #RansomHub #CyberCrime #RaaS #Ransomware
results.hook: A cartel press conference in the dark web is a concept so audacious it demands a second look — corporate evil made cinematic.

results.caption:
DragonForce doesn't hack you. Their affiliates do.

The cartel built a franchise. Affiliates bring the access. DragonForce provides the ransomware, the infrastructure, the negotiation portal, and the brand.

The split? 80% to the affiliate. 20% to the cartel.

This is RaaS maturity. This is organised crime wearing a business suit.

And it's why attribution is so difficult — the person who encrypted your servers may have nothing to do with the person who built the malware.

Know the model. Defend against the model.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #RaaS #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: The franchise model behind ransomware exposes how cybercrime scaled into a billion-dollar industry with corporate profit-sharing — and LinkedIn professionals recognize this business structure instantly.

results.caption:
363 confirmed victims.
212.5% surge in attacks.
35 organisations hit in a single month — December 2025.

DragonForce didn't creep into existence. It exploded.

From its BreachForums debut on December 6, 2023 to becoming one of the most active ransomware cartels on the planet in under 24 months — the growth curve is not a trend. It's an acceleration.

The question isn't whether your sector is targeted. It's whether you'll be victim 364.

Time to check your exposure.

#DragonForce #RansomwareAttack #CyberThreat #IncidentResponse #CyberAwareness
results.hook: A physical scoreboard of ransomware victims with real numbers creates visceral scale that abstract statistics never can — making professionals stop scrolling to count.

results.caption:
30+ countries. Every continent. No sector spared.

DragonForce's affiliates don't discriminate by geography. They discriminate by vulnerability.

🇺🇸 United States — 52% of all victims
🇬🇧 United Kingdom — 12%
🇦🇺 Australia — 6%

And the remaining 30% spread across Europe, Asia-Pacific, Middle East, and beyond.

This isn't a regional threat. This is a global cartel operating with industrial discipline.

If your organisation has internet-facing assets, you are in scope.

#DragonForce #GlobalCyberThreat #RansomwareCartel #CyberResilience #InfoSec
results.hook: A war-room map with physical pins in real countries and percentage callouts makes the global scale of DragonForce tangible and personally threatening to any professional watching from any nation.

results.caption:
Your EDR didn't fail. DragonForce weaponised your OS against it.

BYOVD — Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver.

DragonForce loads a legitimate, Microsoft-signed driver — truesight.sys or rentdrv2.sys — into the Windows kernel. Because it's signed, the OS trusts it completely.

Then they use it to terminate every EDR and AV process on the machine from kernel level.

Your security tools never see the attack coming. By the time ChaCha8 encryption starts, your defences are already dead.

MITRE T1562.001 — Impair Defenses.

This is why layered defence and kernel-level telemetry matter more than ever.

#BYOVD #EDR #DragonForce #MITREAttack #CyberDefense
results.hook: Showing a trusted security tool physically destroyed by malware code that exploits the operating system's own kernel trust model is the most alarming technical concept a security professional can see visualised.

results.caption:
No exploit. No CVE. Just a phone call.

DragonForce didn't just build malware. They partnered with Scattered Spider — a social engineering collective so effective they convinced IT helpdesks to hand over credentials to production systems.

Vishing. Impersonation. SIM swapping. MFA fatigue attacks.

They called your helpdesk. Pretended to be an employee. Got a password reset. Then DragonForce walked in through the front door.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. Summer 2025. £300M+ in total impact.

The most sophisticated cyberattack vector is a confident voice on a telephone.

Train your people. Verify every identity. Trust nothing by voice alone.

#ScatteredSpider #SocialEngineering #Vishing #DragonForce #HumanRisk
results.hook: Showing a threat actor using a telephone and psychological manipulation — not malware — to defeat enterprise security collapses the myth that technical tools alone can protect organisations.

results.caption:
For 9 days, DragonForce operators moved freely through corporate networks — account to account, server to server — while every security tool stayed silent. This is what BYOVD looks like from the inside. Truesight.sys didn't just bypass your EDR. It blinded it. 363 victims later, the question isn't whether your defenses can detect this. It's whether they already failed to. 🐉 

#DragonForce #RansomwareDefense #LateralMovement #CybersecurityAwareness #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: The image of a lone attacker ghost-walking through a corporate network for 9 days without a single alert triggers every security professional's deepest fear.

results.caption:
6 terabytes. Payroll files. Customer databases. M&A documents. IP archives. DragonForce didn't just encrypt your data — they took it first. Double extortion means even if you restore from backup, they still own you. The exfiltration happened quietly, over days, disguised as normal traffic. By the time encryption began, the real damage was already done. This is why perimeter detection alone will never be enough. 📤🐉

#DataExfiltration #DragonForce #DoubleExtortion #DataProtection #CyberResilience
results.hook: A visualization of 6 terabytes of corporate data silently streaming out of an organization while staff work normally creates visceral dread and immediate shareability.

results.caption:
Your backups were your safety net. DragonForce knew that. They got there first. Before encryption began, operators systematically identified and destroyed backup repositories — shadow copies deleted, NAS devices wiped, tape systems overwritten. When the ransom note appeared, there was nothing to restore from. This is T1490. This is why air-gapped, immutable backups are non-negotiable. Don't let your last hope become their first target. 💾🔥

#BackupSecurity #DragonForce #RansomwareRecovery #IncidentResponse #CyberSecurity
results.hook: The image of burning backup tapes and smashed recovery servers — the moment an organization realizes they have no way back — is emotionally devastating and professionally urgent.

results.caption:
ChaCha8. Cross-platform. Simultaneous. Windows endpoints. Linux servers. VMware ESXi hypervisors. DragonForce's encryption engine doesn't discriminate — it was engineered to take everything down at once, leaving no fallback environment standing. In December 2025, 35 organizations were hit in a single month. 35 incident response teams spinning up at the same time. The encryption wave doesn't announce itself. It just arrives. Are your ESXi hosts as protected as your endpoints? 🔐🌊

#ChaCha8 #ESXiRansomware #DragonForce #CrossPlatform #RansomwareAttack
results.hook: A massive encryption wave crashing through a digital cityscape — showing ChaCha8 simultaneously hitting Windows, Linux, and ESXi — demonstrates the true cross-platform scale of modern ransomware.

results.caption:
The encryption was just the beginning. Then the phone rang. DragonForce partnered with Scattered Spider — notorious social engineers — to pressure victims directly. Executives received personal calls. IT staff were manipulated into handing over credentials. Employees were targeted at home. This wasn't just ransomware. This was a coordinated psychological operation designed to break your will to resist. Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. £300 million in impact. The cartel didn't just attack systems. They attacked people. 📞😰

#ScatteredSpider #Vishing #DragonForce #SocialEngineering #HumanRisk
results.hook: A ringing phone in a ransacked executive office — showing the exact moment DragonForce's Scattered Spider affiliates make their vishing call — is one of the most humanly terrifying cybercrime images possible.

results.caption:
On April 8, 2025, DragonForce didn't just recruit — they consumed an entire ransomware operation overnight.

RansomHub. Gone. Absorbed. Rebranded under the Dragon.

This isn't hacking. This is cartel capitalism.

One announcement on a dark web forum. Hundreds of new affiliates. A single criminal brand now controlling a massive slice of global ransomware revenue.

The 80/20 split made it irresistible: affiliates keep 80% of every ransom paid.

This is why DragonForce grew 212.5% in attack volume — they didn't build capacity, they bought it.

#Ransomware #DragonForce #RansomHub #CyberThreatIntelligence #InfoSec
results.hook: The visual metaphor of a corporate hostile takeover executed in darkness forces security leaders to confront how ransomware cartels now grow like legitimate businesses.

results.caption:
Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods.

Three of Britain's most trusted names. One summer. One cartel.

DragonForce — operating through Scattered Spider's vishing expertise — didn't just encrypt files.

They emptied loyalty databases. Froze supply chains. Killed online orders for weeks.

The bill? Over £300 million in confirmed impact across Summer 2025.

This is what a ransomware cartel looks like when it targets a specific geography, a specific sector, with surgical social engineering.

No zero-day needed. Just a phone call.

#DragonForce #UKCyberAttack #Ransomware #ScatteredSpider #RetailSecurity
results.hook: Seeing iconic British retail brands reduced to ransom victims with a £300M price tag makes the abstract threat devastatingly real for every business leader in the UK and beyond.

results.topic: Full MITRE Kill Chain — All 10 Steps
results.caption:
Here is exactly how DragonForce kills a network — step by step — mapped to MITRE ATT&CK.

Step 1: Initial Access via vishing (T1566.004)
Step 2: Credential theft from helpdesk (T1078)
Step 3: VPN/RDP lateral movement (T1021.001)
Step 4: BYOVD — truesight.sys loads (T1068)
Step 5: EDR termination — rentdrv2.sys (T1562.001)
Step 6: Privilege escalation to SYSTEM (T1068)
Step 7: AD enumeration + LSASS dump (T1003.001)
Step 8: Data exfiltration pre-encryption (T1041)
Step 9: ChaCha8 encryption deployed (T1486)
Step 10: Ransom demand delivered (T1491.002)

Knowing the chain is the first step to breaking it.

#MITREATTACK #DragonForce #KillChain #ThreatHunting #BlueTeam
results.hook: Mapping every MITRE ATT&CK technique DragonForce uses onto a single visceral visual makes this the definitive reference image every SOC analyst will save, share, and pin to their wall.

results.caption:
DragonForce has a kill chain. It also has kill switches — if you know where to put them.

Here are the 5 defenses that directly break their attack pattern:

🛡️ 1. PHISHING-RESISTANT MFA — kills credential theft before it starts
🛡️ 2. BYOVD DRIVER BLOCKLISTING — stops truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys cold
🛡️ 3. EDR WITH KERNEL PROTECTION — survives their termination attempts
🛡️ 4. IMMUTABLE OFFLINE BACKUPS — neutralizes ChaCha8 encryption leverage
🛡️ 5. VISHING-AWARE HELPDESK TRAINING — closes the Scattered Spider door

None of these require a $10M budget.
All of them were absent in Summer 2025's biggest retail breaches.

Defend accordingly.

#CyberDefense #DragonForce #RansomwareDefense #ZeroTrust #SecurityAwareness
results.hook: Giving security teams a visually iconic, immediately actionable defense framework transforms a threat brief into a tactical asset that gets shared across entire organizations.

results.topic: The Dragon Is Already Inside The Walls
results.caption:
You're looking for the breach.

You're checking perimeter logs. Reviewing firewall rules. Running external scans.

But DragonForce doesn't come through the wall.

They come through the phone. Through the helpdesk. Through your own credentials.

By the time your EDR would have fired — it's already dead. truesight.sys saw to that.

By the time you notice unusual network traffic — the data is already gone. T1041 ran quietly for 72 hours.

By the time you see the ransom note — 847,293 files are encrypted. ChaCha8 doesn't unlock.

363 organizations learned this the hard way.

Don't be 364.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #ThreatIntelligence #IncidentResponse #CISO
results.hook: A sweeping cinematic landscape revealing the dragon coiled within a corporate network — already past every defense — creates the visceral dread that motivates immediate action from C-suite to SOC.
